One year ago today I was roughly three weeks postpartum and developing a severe headache.
It began as a mild annoyance but grew progressively worse as the day wore on. 
Since I had been diagnosed with pre-eclampsia with severe features I knew to be wary of such a symptom after giving birth. My home blood pressure cuff gave me a high reading and then a normal reading. I didn’t know which to believe.
That night after putting the baby to bed I took some Tylenol. When she awoke a short time later I was unable to care for her. It felt like my brain was squeezing out of my skull. My eyes and nose were running as if my brain were leaking out (that’s how it felt). My actions felt disjointed as if my brain and body were cut off from one other. I laid back down and it became increasingly difficult to keep my eyes open or to speak. Then I began to throw up. I remember calling my parents as my husband tended to the baby and tried to help me at the same time. All I could mumble on the phone was “help me, help me”. I described it at the time as feeling like I was heading into a coma as if everything were shutting down.
Eventually I’d find myself hospitalized for a Cerebral Venous Sinus Thrombosis. Medical speak for a blood clot in my brain. These are sinuses for blood with giant veins inside, not mucus sinuses. The clot begins in the superior sagittal sinus and extends down through the transverse and sigmoid sinuses on both sides. It is a potentially deadly and rare form of stroke though they caught mine before I stroked or hemorrhaged. Thankfully it responded to treatment and I was released 9/11/24. I am still undergoing treatment and testing one year later. The clot is still here, it is now described as “chronic and stable”.
I’ve been cleared of genetic abnormalities and it seems that on top of being postpartum with pregnancy complications, I also became very dehydrated and immobile. Those factors directly contributed to the clot forming.

One thing I’ve learned through this experience is that God is all that truly matters. He tells us to not be afraid. I was very afraid to be quite honest. But I’ve since grown spiritually to realize that if I’m in a state of grace, then even death is nothing to fear. Beforehand, I intellectually understood that but having to live it is another matter. I also learned that if you’re not feeling right, listen to your body.

Take care and God bless.

One year ago today I was roughly three weeks postpartum and developing a severe headache.
It began as a mild annoyance but grew progressively worse as the day wore on. 
Since I had been diagnosed with pre-eclampsia with severe features I knew to be wary of such a symptom after giving birth. My home blood pressure cuff gave me a high reading and then a normal reading. I didn’t know which to believe.
That night after putting the baby to bed I took some Tylenol. When she awoke a short time later I was unable to care for her. It felt like my brain was squeezing out of my skull. My eyes and nose were running as if my brain were leaking out (that’s how it felt). My actions felt disjointed as if my brain and body were cut off from one other. I laid back down and it became increasingly difficult to keep my eyes open or to speak. Then I began to throw up. I remember calling my parents as my husband tended to the baby and tried to help me at the same time. All I could mumble on the phone was “help me, help me”. I described it at the time as feeling like I was heading into a coma as if everything were shutting down.
Eventually I’d find myself hospitalized for a Cerebral Venous Sinus Thrombosis. Medical speak for a blood clot in my brain. These are sinuses for blood with giant veins inside, not mucus sinuses. The clot begins in the superior sagittal sinus and extends down through the transverse and sigmoid sinuses on both sides. It is a potentially deadly and rare form of stroke though they caught mine before I stroked or hemorrhaged. Thankfully it responded to treatment and I was released 9/11/24. I am still undergoing treatment and testing one year later. The clot is still here, it is now described as “chronic and stable”.
I’ve been cleared of genetic abnormalities and it seems that on top of being postpartum with pregnancy complications, I also became very dehydrated and immobile. Those factors directly contributed to the clot forming.

One thing I’ve learned through this experience is that God is all that truly matters. He tells us to not be afraid. I was very afraid to be quite honest. But I’ve since grown spiritually to realize that if I’m in a state of grace, then even death is nothing to fear. Beforehand, I intellectually understood that but having to live it is another matter. I also learned that if you’re not feeling right, listen to your body.

Take care and God bless.
